key_insight: A physics-informed CNN-LSTM can forecast long-term photoluminescence decay in metal halide perovskites from early degradation signals, enabling stability prediction for unseen samples.
engineering_relevance: This could substantially shorten materials qualification cycles by replacing some prolonged heat-and-light ageing tests with data-driven forecasts that remain interpretable.
method: Collected 167 metal halide perovskite samples with varied compositions and crystallinities.
Stressed samples under elevated temperature and illumination while recording more than 86,000 PL spectra.
Converted spectra into physics-informed features and trained a hybrid CNN-LSTM to predict future PL intensity.
evidence_signal: The model was evaluated on samples unseen during training and is reported to outperform baseline benchmarks while generalizing across material groups.
watch_item: Forecast reliability may depend on the representativeness of the training dataset and on whether new materials fall within the same degradation regimes.
paper_title: Physics-informed time-series forecasting of perovskite photoluminescence stability
